policy_statement: Lebanon commits to unconditionally generate 18% of electricity (power demand) and 11% of building-sector heat demand from renewable energy sources by 2030.
verbatim_text: Moreover,  Lebanon  commits  to  unconditionally  generate  18%  of  the  power  demand  (i.e.  electricity demand) and 11% of its heat demand (in the building sector) from renewable energy sources in 2030, compared to a combined 15% in 2015.
extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable energy shares with a deadline (2030).
reasoning: The statement is an auditable national commitment with clear, measurable renewable energy share targets (18% electricity demand and 11% building heat demand) and a specific deadline (2030). The language (“commits to unconditionally generate”) is strong and not merely aspirational, making it actionable and measurable under the strict rule.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Yes — text states “generate 18% of the power demand … and 11% of its heat demand (in the building sector) from renewable energy sources”. Timeline: Yes — “in 2030”. Binding mechanism: No — the excerpt is a commitment statement and does not specify an enforceable law/ordinance/mandate. Spatial specificity: Yes — applies to “Lebanon” and specifies sectoral scope (“building sector”). Weak language: No — no ‘encourage/aim/explore’ phrasing; it uses commitment language. Strong language: Yes — “commits to unconditionally generate” paired with hard percentages and a year. Strict valid rule check: (1) Auditable deliverable present (renewable shares of demand), (2) Gold standard met (timeline + quantifiable targets), (3) No weak language, so passes even without binding mechanism.

policy_statement: Lebanon commits to reduce power demand through energy-efficiency measures by 3% under BAU in 2030 unconditionally, and by 10% under BAU in 2030 conditionally.
verbatim_text:
3. A 3% reduction in power demand through energy-efficiency measures in 2030 compared to the demand under the BAU scenario.
...
3. A 10% reduction in power demand through energy-efficiency in 2030 compared to the demand under the BAU scenario.
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified energy-efficiency targets tied to BAU and a target year (2030).
reasoning: The verbatim text contains explicit, measurable outcome targets (3% and 10% reduction in power demand relative to BAU) and an explicit deadline year (2030). This is an auditable commitment in the energy sector (demand reduction via efficiency). However, the excerpt does not itself establish an enforceable legal/administrative mechanism (e.g., mandate/ordinance), nor does it specify a geographic scope beyond the implied national context (“Lebanon” appears only in the policy_statement, not the verbatim_text provided). Language is target-oriented rather than purely aspirational.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text specifies measurable reductions: 'A 3% reduction in power demand' and 'A 10% reduction in power demand' 'compared to the demand under the BAU scenario.' Timeline: Both targets are explicitly for 'in 2030.' Binding mechanism: No ordinance/mandate/regulation or enforcement instrument is stated in the excerpt, so binding authority is not evidenced. Spatial specificity: The excerpt does not state where it applies (no 'Lebanon', 'national', 'citywide', etc. in the provided verbatim_text), so this criterion is not met. Weak language: The statements are framed as direct targets ('A X% reduction... in 2030') rather than 'encourage/aim/explore', so weak language is not detected. Strong language: Presence of hard numbers and a deadline ('3%... in 2030', '10%... in 2030') indicates strong, target-setting language.
